LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT WESTERN DENTAL SERVICES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT WESTERN DENTAL SERVICES, INC. HAS NOT MET ITS INITIAL BURDEN TO DEMONSTRATE THAT PLAINTIFF ASHWIN NANDA CLAIMS IN THE PRESENT ACTION ARE BARRED BY RES JUDICATA. DEFENDANT HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT THE CLAIMS OR ISSUES RAISED IN THE PRESENT ACTION ARE IDENTICAL TO THE CLAIMS OR ISSUES LITIGATED IN NANDA V. WESTERN DENTAL SERVICES (N.D. CAL. CASE NO. C 12-04654-PJH). (BOEKEN V. PHILIP MORRIS USA, INC. (2010) 48 CAL.4TH 788, 797.) IN THE PRESENT ACTION, PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARE BASED ON MISCLASSIFICATION AS AN EXEMPT EMPLOYEE FOR WAGE AND HOUR VIOLATIONS. (SEE REQ. JUD. NOTICE, EXHIBITS B, C.) IN THE FEDERAL ACTION, PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS ARE BASED ON VIOLATION OF TITLE VII OF THE CIVIL RIGHTS ACT FOR EMPLOYMENT DISCRIMINATION. (REQ. JUD. NOTICE, EXHIBIT H.) THESE TWO ACTIONS DO NOT INVOLVE THE SAME PRIMARY RIGHT. (BOEKEN, SUPRA, 48 CAL.4TH AT 797-98. SEE ALSO EICHMAN V. FOTOMAT CORP. (1983) 147 CAL.APP.3D 1170, 1174.) DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. PLAINTIFF ASHWIN NANDA'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED. KONING & ASSOCIATES (2013) 216 CAL.APP.4TH 392 ADDRESSED THE ADMINISTRATIVE EXEMPTION, NOT THE PROFESSIONAL EXEMPTION AS IT IS APPLIED TO DENTISTS. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Summ Adj; Appendix
Off Calendar Feb-11-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DAVID SOHN OF THE JOHN LEGAL GROUP, TEL. NO. 421-1300 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MAIKO NAKARAI-KANIVAS, ATTY. SOPHIA BAHNIA & ATTY. MICHELLE HEVERLY OF THE LITTLER LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 288-6341 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANT WESTERN DENTAL SERVICES, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication - Motion for Summary Judgment is Denied. Defendant Western Dental Services, Inc. has not met its initial burden to demonstrate that Plaintiff Ashwin Nanda claims in the present action are barred by res judicata. Defendant has not demonstrated that the claims or issues raised in the present action are identical to the claims or issues litigated in Nanda v. Western Dental Services (N.D. Cal. Case no. C 12-04654-PJH). (Boeken v. Philip Morris USA, Inc. (2010) 48 Cal.4th 788, 797.) In the present action, Plaintiff's claims are based on misclassification as an exempt employee for wage and hour violations. (See Req. Jud. Notice, Exhibits B, C.) In the federal action, Plaintiff's claims are based on violation of Title VII of the Civil Rights Act for employment discrimination. (Req. Jud. Notice, Exhibit H.) These two actions do not involve the same primary right. (Boeken, supra, 48 Cal.4th at 797-98. See also Eichman v. Fotomat Corp. (1983) 147 Cal.App.3d 1170, 1174.) Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/EHG

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Feb-11-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. DAVID SOHN OF THE JOHN LEGAL GROUP, TEL. NO. 421-1300 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MAIKO NAKARAI-KANIVAS, ATTY. SOPHIA BAHNIA & ATTY. MICHELLE HEVERLY OF THE LITTLER LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 288-6341 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF ASHWIN NANDA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is Denied. Plaintiff fails to produce any on point authority supporting the contention that he is a non-exempt employee. Negri v. Koning & Associates (2013) 216 Cal.App.4th 392 addressed the administrative exemption, not the professional exemption as it is applied to dentists. The definition of "salary base" found in 29 C.F.R. 541.602 do not apply to dentists. (29 C.F.R. 541.304(d).) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EHG)
